Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Johan Kugelberg hip hop collection, 1975-2008
Collection Number: 8021

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Johan Kugelberg hip hop collection, 1975-2008
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
8021
Creator:
Kugelberg, Johan
Johan, Kugelberg
Conzo, Joe, Jr.
Afrika Bambaataa, 1957-
Quanitities:
22 cubic feet.
Language:
In English.
Scope and content

Contains nearly 1,000 recordings (primarily LP records), textile art, books and magazines, and more than 500 original party and club flyers designed by Buddy Esquire and others. The collection documents the origins of hip-hop as music, culture and community in the Bronx, NY, and its influence on the history of music, art, performance, and activism in America during the final third of the 20th century and beyond.
Immediate Source of Acquisition

Source of acquisition--The founding materials in the collection were the gift of collector and author Johan Kugelberg..
Scope and content

Also, t-shirts, tote bag, and other memorabilia documenting the 2008 Hip Hop Conference and Celebration at Cornell; and a poster for Spike Lee's motion picture "Do the Right Thing" (1989).
Immediate Source of Acquisition

Source of acquisition--The founding materials in the collection were the gift of collector and author Johan Kugelberg..
Scope and content

74 candid hip hop photos depicting various music events, record signings and performances - includes Raandu Avion, Brandy, Missy Elliott, Mary J. Blige, DMX and Wyclef Jean (2001).
Immediate Source of Acquisition

Source of acquisition--The founding materials in the collection were the gift of collector and author Johan Kugelberg..
Conditions Governing Use

Sound recordings on vinyl may be requested and viewed, but not played.
Conditions Governing Use

Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.
Conditions Governing Use

Use of digitized audio visual materials is limited to on-site access only.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Preferred Citation

Johan Kugelberg hip hop collection, #8021. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Immediate Source of Acquisition

Source of acquisition--The founding materials in the collection were the gift of collector and author Johan Kugelberg..
Publications About Described Materials

Described in: Born in the Bronx: a visual record of the early days of hip hop / edited by Johan Kugelberg; photographs by Joe Conzo; foreword by Afrika Bambaataa; original flyer art by Buddy Esquire; featuring a timeline by Jeff Chang. New York: Rizzoli, 2007.

V-1292
Beat Street / an Orion Pictures release; Harry Belafonte & David V Picker present; associate producer, Mel Howard; story by Steve Hager; screenplay by Andy David & David Gilbert & Paul Golding; produced by David V Picker & Harry Belafonte; directed by Stan Lathan.
 1984
Scope and Contents
Publisher: ORION Pictures Corp.
Featuring: Rae Dawn Chong (Tracy). Guy Davis (Kenny). Jon Chardiet (Ramon). Leon W. Grant (Chollie). Saundra Santiago (Carmen). And introducing Robert Taylor as Lee. Lee Chamberlin (Alicia), Mary Alice (Smith) (Cora), Shawn Elliot (Domingo). Jim Borrelli (Monte), Dean Elliott (Henry), Franc Reyes (Luis). And also starring, in alphabetical order, Afrika Bambaataa and the Soul Sonic Force + Shango. Tina B. Andy B. Bad. Fantastic Duo. Bernard Fowler. Grand Master Melle Mel and the Furious Five. Jazzy Jay (Roxy D.J.). Magnificent Force. New York City Breakers. Rock Steady Crew. Richard Sisco and Wanda Dee. Brenda Starr. The System. The Treacherous Three. Us Girls. Tonya Pinkins (Angela); Antonia Rey (Flora); Duane Jones (Robert); Hope Clarke (dancing instructor). Kool Herc (Clive Campbell) (himself); Gina Belafonte (Elizabeth); Kimry Smith (Erika); Douglas Davis (Douggie Fresh); Richard Thomsen (Paul); Kadeem Hardison (high school student). Tony Lopez (Pex) (Beat Street breaker); Lorenzo Soto (Kuriaki) (Bronx rocker); Dadi Pinero (young writer); Bill Anagnos (stunt player); Pedro B. Serrano (Pedro); Mervyn E. Griffith (student); Joseph Verhauz (transit policeman) ... [etc.]
